vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1141: Agent auto-resume controls — emergency mode, issue-level pause, troubled-state retry limits

plan.title: Agent auto-resume controls: emergency mode, issue-level pause, troubled-state retry limits
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

references.label: PAN-1140 (related: container init fix; troubled flag's first real use case)

narratives.Problem: After a reboot or `pan dev`/`pan up` restart, Panopticon's deacon auto-resumes every in-flight work agent indiscriminately. There is no way to (1) boot the dashboard cleanly for inspection without spawning agents, (2) hold a specific issue's agent across restarts while waiting on review or human input, or (3) stop a thrashing agent from being respawned forever. A real incident produced agents with 39–98 hours of accumulated duration silently burning tokens across multiple reboots.
narratives.Proposal: Add three orthogonal gating mechanisms on top of the existing `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents()` loop in `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:4767` and the orphan-recovery patrol at `:4264`: (1) a per-boot `PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME=1` env var / `--no-resume` flag on `pan dev` and `pan up` that short-circuits BOTH orphan recovery and auto-resume; (2) a persistent per-issue `paused` flag on the agent state JSON, settable via `pan pause <id>` and clearable via `pan unpause <id>`, that survives reboots; (3) per-agent consecutive-failure tracking with exponential backoff (5s/30s/2min) that flips the agent to `troubled` after 3 failures in 10 minutes (configurable per project), clearable via `pan untroubled <id>`. The deacon checks all three gates before resuming any agent. Dashboard surfaces all three states (top-level no-resume banner, paused/troubled badges, single 'why didn't this auto-start?' pill). All three flags persist on the existing JSON state at `~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/state.json` (not SQLite — that's a misread of the issue body; agent state is file-based today and the persistence requirement is met by JSON files surviving reboots).

autoDecisions.summary: Verb naming: `pan pause` / `pan unpause` / `pan untroubled` rather than `pan resume`
autoDecisions.rationale: `pan resume <id>` already exists in `src/cli/commands/resume.ts` and resumes a stopped Claude Code session. Reusing the verb would silently change semantics. `pause`/`unpause` is the natural symmetric pair for a persistent flag; `untroubled` matches the issue body's suggestion.

autoDecisions.summary: Storage: extend the existing JSON `state.json` rather than introduce a SQLite agents table
autoDecisions.rationale: Agent state today lives in `~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/state.json` (src/lib/agents.ts:525). SQLite holds review_status/cost_events/health_events but not agent runtime state. JSON files persist across reboots/restarts so the issue body's persistence requirement is met without a schema migration. Adding a new SQLite table just for these three flags would split the source of truth for agent state.

autoDecisions.summary: `pan pause <id>` stops the running agent AND sets the persistent flag
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue framing ('useful when an agent is misbehaving') strongly implies the user wants the live agent halted, not just blocked from future auto-resume. A pause that leaves a misbehaving agent running for several more minutes until it self-stops would not match user intent.

autoDecisions.summary: `pan unpause <id>` clears the flag only — does NOT auto-spawn
autoDecisions.rationale: Spawning on unpause would surprise users who paused to inspect. The deacon's next patrol cycle picks up unpaused issues that should resume; the user can also run `pan start <id>` explicitly.

autoDecisions.summary: `PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME=1` is per-boot only, not a persistent SQLite flag
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD's 'Boot --no-resume' badge label implies per-boot scope. A persistent flag would silently disable resume forever after one inspection session — a foot-gun. Per-boot scope matches every comparable inspect-mode toggle (e.g. `PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON`).

autoDecisions.summary: Default troubled threshold: 3 consecutive failures within 10 minutes. Default backoff: 5s, 30s, 120s
Per-project overrides live in `projects.yaml` under a new `autoResume:` block
autoDecisions.rationale: Consistent with how other per-project knobs (verification timeouts, harness defaults) are scoped today. Adding a separate config file would fragment configuration; per-issue overrides would have nowhere to live before the issue is even planned.
Gating only the latter would still let orphan recovery reset every dead tmux session to `stopped` on boot — destroying the inspection state the user wanted to preserve. Hazard H4 in continue.json.

autoDecisions.summary: `pan start <id>` refuses by default when paused; `--force` overrides
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body says 'warn (or refuse with --force)' — refuse is safer than warn-and-spawn because the user explicitly paused. `--force` provides the documented escape hatch and must clear the `paused` flag on success.

autoDecisions.summary: Wrapper skills created for `pan-pause`, `pan-unpause`, `pan-untroubled` (not on the exclusion list)
autoDecisions.rationale: These are new lifecycle controls with non-trivial semantics (persistence, interaction with the deacon, --force escape hatches). The skills convention's exclusion list is for trivial verbs whose --help is self-explanatory; these have judgment calls (when to pause vs kill, when to untroubled vs investigate root cause) that a wrapper skill should encode.

items.title: Extend AgentState with pause/troubled/failure-tracking fields and helper functions

narrative.Action: Add fields `paused`, `pausedReason`, `pausedAt`, `troubled`, `troubledAt`, `consecutiveFailures`, `firstFailureInRunAt`, `lastFailureAt`, `lastFailureReason`, `lastFailureNextRetryAt` to `AgentState` (src/lib/agents.ts:525) and `cleanAgentState()` (~line 594). Add helpers in `src/lib/agents.ts`: `setAgentPaused(agentId, reason?)`, `clearAgentPaused(agentId)`, `markAgentTroubled(agentId)`, `clearAgentTroubled(agentId)`, `recordAgentFailure(agentId, reason)`, `resetAgentFailureCount(agentId)`, `isAgentPaused(agentId)`, `isAgentTroubled(agentId)`. Helpers must use the existing `getAgentState`/`saveAgentState` pattern and be safe to call when state.json does not exist (return false / no-op). This bead is the foundation every other bead depends on — interface naming and helper signatures encoded here propagate everywhere.

subItems.title: All ten new fields are present on `AgentState` and round-trip through `cleanAgentState()` without loss
All eight new helpers exist, are exported from `src/lib/agents.ts`, and have JSDoc explaining when each is called
Helpers are idempotent: calling `setAgentPaused` twice does not change `pausedAt`; calling `clearAgentPaused` on an unpaused agent is a no-op
Helpers handle the missing-state.json case gracefully (return false / no-op) without throwing
`npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass

items.title: Gate autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents() on paused and troubled flags

narrative.Action: In `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:4767` `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents()`, add skip clauses for `state.paused === true` and `state.troubled === true`. Place these ABOVE the `hasPendingReviewFeedback` block — a paused or troubled agent should NEVER be auto-resumed even when review feedback is pending (the human paused/troubled it for a reason). Each skip logs via `logDeaconEvent()` with a clear reason string.

subItems.title: Paused agents are skipped with log line `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents: <id> skipped — paused (<reason>)`
Troubled agents are skipped with log line `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents: <id> skipped — troubled (<N> consecutive failures since <timestamp>)`
Both gates take precedence over the review-feedback short-circuit — a paused/troubled agent with pending review feedback is still NOT resumed

items.title: Honor PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME=1 in deacon startup paths

narrative.Action: Add `PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME` env check at the top of BOTH `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents()` (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:4767) and `recoverOrphanedAgents()` (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:4264). When set to a truthy value (`1`/`true`/`yes`), log `[deacon] PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME=1 — skipping <function>` and return early. Expose the no-resume state on the dashboard server: a module-level boolean in `src/dashboard/server/main.ts` populated at boot from `process.env.PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME`, plus a read-only `GET /api/no-resume-mode` endpoint returning `{ active: boolean, since: string | null }`.

subItems.title: `PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME=1` causes `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents()` to return an empty array without scanning agent dirs
`PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME=1` causes `recoverOrphanedAgents()` to return without touching any state.json
Dashboard exposes `GET /api/no-resume-mode` returning the active state and boot timestamp
Truthy values `1`, `true`, `yes` are all honored; falsy values `0`, `false`, empty string disable

items.title: Increment and reset the consecutive-failure counter at cloister failure sites

narrative.Action: Call `recordAgentFailure(agentId, reason)` from two sites: (a) inside `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents()` when `resumeAgent()` returns `{success:false}` or throws (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:4906/4912); (b) inside `recoverOrphanedAgents()` when a tmux session is detected missing AND `stoppedByUser !== true` (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:4264 region — the orphan crash case). Call `resetAgentFailureCount(agentId)` from a new place: the post-spawn success path where the agent has reached `running` status. Do NOT reset on `resumeAgent` returning success alone — wait until status is `running` to avoid resetting when the spawn pipeline itself fails mid-way. Ensure the orphan-recovery + auto-resume call sequence in one patrol cycle increments exactly ONCE per detected crash.

subItems.title: Failed auto-resume increments `consecutiveFailures` by 1 and stamps `lastFailureAt` / `lastFailureReason`
Orphan-crash detection increments `consecutiveFailures` exactly once per detected crash (no double-count when orphan-recovery and auto-resume run in the same patrol cycle)
Successful spawn (agent reaches `running`) resets `consecutiveFailures` to 0 and clears `firstFailureInRunAt`/`lastFailureAt`/`lastFailureReason`/`lastFailureNextRetryAt`

items.title: Exponential backoff and threshold-based troubled flip

narrative.Action: After incrementing the counter in `recordAgentFailure()`, compute `lastFailureNextRetryAt = now + backoffSchedule[min(consecutiveFailures-1, schedule.length-1)] * 1000`. Default `backoffSchedule = [5, 30, 120]` (seconds). Track `firstFailureInRunAt`: set on the first failure of a run, cleared on counter reset. When `consecutiveFailures >= maxConsecutiveFailures` AND `(now - firstFailureInRunAt) <= troubledWindowMs`, call `markAgentTroubled()`. If the window has elapsed without hitting the threshold, reset `firstFailureInRunAt` to the current failure timestamp. Default thresholds: `maxConsecutiveFailures = 3`, `troubledWindowMs = 600000` (10 min). Add a skip clause in `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents()` that skips agents whose `lastFailureNextRetryAt > Date.now()` (inclusive equality means retry-now). Create `src/lib/cloister/auto-resume-config.ts` exporting `AutoResumeConfig` interface and `DEFAULT_AUTO_RESUME_CONFIG` for reuse by tests and config overrides.

subItems.title: After 1st failure, agent is skipped during the next 5 seconds and resumable after; after 2nd, 30s; after 3rd, 120s
3 consecutive failures within 10 minutes (default config) flip `troubled = true` and stamp `troubledAt`
If failures span > 10 minutes (e.g. 1 failure now, 2 more 11 minutes later), no troubled-flip happens — `firstFailureInRunAt` slides to the failure that resets the window
`AutoResumeConfig` interface and defaults exported from `src/lib/cloister/auto-resume-config.ts`
Skipping during backoff logs `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents: <id> skipped — backoff active (next retry at <iso>)`

narrative.Action: Create `src/cli/commands/pause.ts` following the `kill.ts` pattern (src/cli/commands/kill.ts). Behavior: resolve issue ID → `agentId = agent-<issueid>` → if running, call `stopAgent(agentId)` (same shutdown path as `pan kill`) → call `setAgentPaused(agentId, options.reason)`. Output a chalk-green confirmation including the reason. Wire into `src/cli/index.ts` alongside the existing imports.

subItems.title: `pan pause <id>` succeeds when the agent is running (kills it) AND when it is already stopped (just sets the flag)
`--reason "text"` writes the reason to `pausedReason`; absence omits the field
Re-pausing an already-paused agent updates the reason but does NOT overwrite `pausedAt`
Unknown agent ID prints a clear error and exits non-zero

narrative.Action: Create `src/cli/commands/unpause.ts`. Resolve issue ID → call `clearAgentPaused(agentId)` → output `chalk.green` confirmation. Does NOT spawn the agent — print a dim hint suggesting `pan start <id>` if the user wants to resume immediately, or note that the deacon's next patrol will pick it up.

subItems.title: `pan unpause <id>` clears `paused`, `pausedReason`, `pausedAt` from state.json
Unpausing a non-paused agent is a successful no-op with a dim 'already unpaused' message
Does NOT spawn or resume the agent; output includes the hint to run `pan start` if desired

narrative.Action: Create `src/cli/commands/untroubled.ts`. Resolve issue ID → call `clearAgentTroubled(agentId)` which also calls `resetAgentFailureCount()`. Output a chalk-green confirmation. Does NOT auto-spawn — same rationale as unpause.

subItems.title: `pan untroubled <id>` clears `troubled`, `troubledAt`, `consecutiveFailures`, `firstFailureInRunAt`, `lastFailureAt`, `lastFailureReason`, `lastFailureNextRetryAt`
Untroubling a non-troubled agent is a successful no-op with a dim message
Does NOT spawn or resume the agent

narrative.Action: In `src/cli/commands/dev.ts`, add Commander `--no-resume` option (default false). When set, inject `PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME=1` into the child dashboard-server process's environment. Surface in the startup banner that no-resume mode is active.

subItems.title: `pan dev --no-resume` starts dashboard + traefik + cliproxy normally
Child dashboard process sees `PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME=1` in its environment
No agents are auto-spawned; deacon logs the skip; dashboard shows the no-resume banner
Startup terminal output shows a clear `[no-resume mode active]` line

narrative.Action: In `src/cli/index.ts` `pan up` definition (around line 810-830), add the `--no-resume` flag with identical semantics to the `pan dev` flag — inject `PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME=1` into the dashboard process env.

subItems.title: `pan up --no-resume` boots the dashboard without spawning agents
Startup terminal output includes a `[no-resume mode active]` line
`pan up` without the flag continues to auto-resume agents (no regression)

narrative.Action: In `src/cli/commands/status.ts`, for each non-running in-flight agent, compute a `gatingReason` string: `Paused (<reason>)`, `Troubled (<N> failures)`, `Boot --no-resume`, `Manual`, or empty if the agent is mid-pipeline. Add a column or per-agent line showing it. Boot mode comes from a small helper that checks `process.env.PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME` OR queries the dashboard's `/api/no-resume-mode` endpoint when available.

subItems.title: Each non-running in-flight agent shows one of the four gating reasons (or no gating reason if it is mid-pipeline waiting on review feedback)
Running agents do not show a gating reason column

narrative.Action: In the `pan start` command (find via the import list in `src/cli/index.ts`), before spawning: if `isAgentPaused(agentId)` returns true and `--force` was not passed, print a chalk-red error including the pause reason and exit 1. When `--force` IS passed, clear `paused`/`pausedReason`/`pausedAt` first, then proceed to spawn.

subItems.title: `pan start <id>` on a paused agent prints a clear error referencing `pan unpause` and the pause reason, then exits non-zero
`pan start <id> --force` on a paused agent clears the paused flag and spawns normally
`pan start <id>` on an unpaused agent is unchanged (no regression)

items.title: Dashboard backend: pause/unpause/untroubled endpoints and snapshot fields

narrative.Action: Add four endpoints in the appropriate `src/dashboard/server/routes/*.ts` (likely `agents.ts`): `POST /api/agents/:id/pause` (body `{ reason?: string }`), `POST /api/agents/:id/unpause`, `POST /api/agents/:id/untroubled`, `GET /api/no-resume-mode`. Each mutating endpoint calls the helpers from B1 and emits a domain event so the read-model and frontend update. Add the ten new fields from B1 to the agent snapshot shape consumed by the frontend (likely in `src/dashboard/server/read-model.ts` and any contracts schema in `packages/contracts`).

subItems.title: POST /api/agents/:id/pause sets the flag, kills running agent, returns 200 with updated state
POST /api/agents/:id/unpause clears the flag and returns 200 (does not spawn)
POST /api/agents/:id/untroubled clears troubled + failure counters, returns 200
GET /api/no-resume-mode returns `{ active: boolean, since: string|null }`
Agent snapshot shape includes the ten new fields; contracts package updated and rebuilt
Each mutating endpoint emits a domain event so the frontend updates without polling
All four endpoints use `execAsync` / `fs/promises` — no `execSync` or sync FS calls (CLAUDE.md rule)

items.title: IssueAgentCard: Paused/Troubled badges and gating-reason pill

narrative.Action: In `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/IssueAgentCard.tsx`, extend the status badge area (lines 39-70 region — `STATUS_COLORS`, `HEALTH_STATE_*`) with `Paused` (gray) and `Troubled` (red) badges. Add a single 'gating reason' pill rendered only when the agent is not running and not stopped-via-completion: one of `Manual`, `Paused`, `Troubled (N failures)`, `Boot --no-resume`. Pill text is computed from agent state + the no-resume-mode value from the dashboard's `GET /api/no-resume-mode`.

subItems.title: Paused badge appears on cards whose agent has `paused: true`, with the reason in a tooltip
Troubled badge appears on cards whose agent has `troubled: true`, with failure count and timestamp in a tooltip
Gating-reason pill displays one of the four labels for non-running in-flight agents
Pill is hidden for running agents and for agents that completed normally

items.title: IssueAgentCard: pause/unpause buttons with reason input

narrative.Action: Add a Pause button to the card's action area for non-paused agents and an Unpause button for paused ones. Pause opens a small inline reason input (or a small modal) and calls `POST /api/agents/:id/pause`. Unpause confirms inline and calls `POST /api/agents/:id/unpause`. Buttons disabled during the in-flight request.

subItems.title: Pause button appears for non-paused agents; clicking it shows reason input and submits
Unpause button appears for paused agents; clicking it sends the request and updates the badge immediately on response
Empty reason is allowed (sends no `reason` field)
Error responses surface as a toast or inline error; button re-enables on failure

narrative.Action: On cards whose agent is `troubled: true`, render a 'Clear troubled state' button (or icon button next to the Troubled badge) that calls `POST /api/agents/:id/untroubled`. After success, the badge disappears immediately.

subItems.title: Button visible only on troubled agents
Click clears the badge and zeroes the failure-count tooltip

narrative.Action: Create `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/NoResumeBanner.tsx` modeled on `DeaconPauseBanner` (sibling in the same dir). Fetches `GET /api/no-resume-mode` on mount + on a WebSocket boot event. When `active: true`, renders an orange/red sticky banner: 'No-resume mode active — agents will not auto-start until you restart without `--no-resume`. Use `pan start <id>` to spawn individually.' Render at the very top of `App.tsx` (src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx:32-34 region), above existing banners.

subItems.title: Banner renders when `active: true` and is hidden when false
Banner copy includes the boot timestamp and the override instruction
Banner sits above DeaconPauseBanner / StoppedAgentsBanner / CodexAuthBanner in the App.tsx render order

items.title: Wrapper skills for pan-pause, pan-unpause, pan-untroubled

narrative.Action: Create `skills/pan-pause/SKILL.md`, `skills/pan-unpause/SKILL.md`, `skills/pan-untroubled/SKILL.md` following the CLI-wrapper shape from `docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md`. Each frontmatter description starts with `pan <verb> [args] — purpose`. Body explains when to invoke vs alternatives (e.g. pause vs kill, untroubled vs investigating root cause). Run `npm run lint` to confirm skill-linter passes.

subItems.title: Three SKILL.md files exist with correct frontmatter and CLI-wrapper shape
Each skill body documents the verb's persistence semantics and interaction with `pan start`/`pan kill`
`scripts/lint-skills.sh` (via `npm run lint`) passes — every flag and arg the skills mention is present on the CLI

narrative.Action: Extend the project config schema (`src/lib/projects.ts` or wherever projects.yaml is parsed) with an optional `autoResume:` block: `maxConsecutiveFailures`, `troubledWindowMs`, `failureBackoffSchedule`. Resolve config in `recordAgentFailure()` by issue → project → autoResume override, falling back to the defaults exported from `src/lib/cloister/auto-resume-config.ts` (B5).

subItems.title: projects.yaml accepts an optional `autoResume` block per project; absent block uses defaults
Failure tracking honors the project's threshold and backoff schedule when present
Invalid config values (negative numbers, non-array schedule) fall back to defaults with a logged warning

narrative.Action: Add a new test file `tests/lib/cloister/auto-resume-gating.test.ts` (mirror the pattern from `tests/lib/work-agent-lifecycle.test.ts`). Cover: (a) paused agent is skipped by `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents()` even with pending review feedback; (b) troubled agent is skipped; (c) `PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME=1` causes both `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents` and `recoverOrphanedAgents` to return empty/no-op; (d) `pan start --force` clears paused and spawns; (e) `pan start` without `--force` refuses on paused.

subItems.title: All five test cases exist and pass under `npm test`
Tests use real temp directories for agent state (not mocked fs) to avoid the kind of mock/prod divergence CLAUDE.md warns about

items.title: Tests: failure counter, backoff windows, troubled-flip threshold

narrative.Action: Add `tests/lib/cloister/failure-tracking.test.ts` covering: (a) counter increments on `resumeAgent` failure; (b) counter increments on orphan-crash detection; (c) counter resets on successful `running` transition; (d) backoff windows (5s/30s/120s) are honored — agent skipped during window, eligible after; (e) inclusive-equality boundary at `lastFailureNextRetryAt === now`; (f) 3 failures within 10 min flip `troubled`; (g) 3 failures spanning > 10 min do NOT flip `troubled` (window slides); (h) per-project config overrides the defaults.

subItems.title: All eight test cases exist and pass under `npm test`
Time-dependent tests use fake timers (vitest's `vi.useFakeTimers()`) to keep them deterministic and fast

narrative.Action: Update CLAUDE.md's auto-resume section to mention the three new gates. Update any deacon / cloister docs (search `docs/` for 'auto-resume', 'cloister', 'recoverOrphanedAgents'). Add a short section in the README or docs/ explaining `PANOPTICON_NO_RESUME` and `pan pause`/`pan unpause`/`pan untroubled`. Confirm `docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md` does NOT need to add the new verbs to its exclusion list (they have wrapper skills).

subItems.title: CLAUDE.md and any relevant docs/* references describe the three new gates
README or docs include a 'troubleshooting an agent that keeps crashing' note that mentions `pan untroubled`
`docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md` exclusion list is unchanged or explicitly notes that pan-pause/unpause/untroubled have wrapper skills

review.notes: pan start bypasses troubled gate; dashboard Start buttons shown for paused/troubled agents; dashboard routes use sync I/O; npm test timeout

history.notes: pan start bypasses troubled gate; dashboard Start buttons shown for paused/troubled agents; dashboard routes use sync I/O; npm test timeout
